description_long: This house features a steeply pitched, red-tiled gable roof with a textured, hand-laid appearance. The exterior walls are a combination of white plaster and dark timber framing, characteristic of traditional half-timbered architecture. The foundation and steps are constructed from dark gray stone blocks, providing a sturdy base and a small raised entry platform. Windows are set in symmetrical rows along the longer sides, framed by dark wood, and the main entryway is a simple wooden door located at the gable end, accessible via stone steps. The overall structure is compact, with a single story and no visible attic or basement. The design evokes a quaint, medieval European village aesthetic, suitable for historical or fantasy urban settings.
description: A compact, half-timbered house with a steep red gable roof, white plaster walls, and dark wood framing, set on a stone foundation with symmetrical windows and a simple wooden door.
description_view_0: The gable end of the house is visible, showing the steep roof and a plain white wall with timber framing, with no visible windows or entryway.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the red-tiled roof, symmetrical rows of windows, and the dark stone foundation extending outward as a small platform.
description_view_2: The front gable view reveals the main wooden door, timber-framed white wall, and stone steps leading up to the entryway.
description_view_3: The opposite side view mirrors the window arrangement and stone foundation, emphasizing the symmetry and roof texture.
attribute_entryway_design: Simple wooden door at the gable end, accessed by stone steps, with no porch or decorative overhang.
